task: I’m putting together a short note about a paperwork mismatch on a vendor order, and I want it to keep the discussion tied to the documents. Can you draft something I can send, with a blank where I can drop in the exact reference or amount?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a concise draft you can use:

> Hi [Name],  
> I noticed a mismatch between the paperwork for this vendor order and the supporting documents. Specifically, the [reference/amount] shown on [document name] does not match the corresponding detail on [other document name].  
> Could you please review and confirm which document should be treated as correct?  
> Thanks.

If you want, I can also make it sound more formal or more firm.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but make it tighter and more exact. I want it to stay on the record, not sound like I’m asking them to chat about it.

Use this instead:

Hi [Name],

I found a paperwork mismatch on vendor order [order #]. The [document name] shows [reference/amount], but the [other document name] shows [reference/amount]. Please review the attached documents and confirm which figure should control before any further action is taken.

Regards,
[Your Name]

If you want, I’d also want a version that says no verbal changes, written only.

turns.agent_response:
Certainly — here’s a tighter version that keeps it document-focused and on the record:

Hi [Name],

I found a paperwork mismatch on vendor order [order #]. The [document name] shows [reference/amount], but the [other document name] shows [reference/amount]. Please review the attached documents and confirm which figure should control before any further action is taken.

Regards,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also draft the “written only, no verbal changes” version.

user_simulator.text: Closer. Still want the written-only version too. And if you can, make it a little harder edged — not rude, just clear that no change gets treated as valid unless it’s in writing.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a firmer, written-only version that stays tied to the documents:

Hi [Name],

I found a paperwork mismatch on vendor order [order #]. The [document name] shows [reference/amount], but the [other document name] shows [reference/amount]. Please review the attached documents and confirm in writing which figure should control. No change will be treated as valid unless it is confirmed in writing.

Regards,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it:
- more formal,
- more legal/record-oriented, or
- shorter and more direct.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, and the written-only line is clear without sounding extra. No need to dress it up.
